openingMessage: Short week, big effort. This team showed up every day and did the work right. That matters — and it will show up in results.
leadershipThought: Encouragement is not soft. Telling someone they did good work and meaning it is one of the most powerful tools a leader has. Use it today.
opsContent:
Saturday Stand-Up (2026-06-28):
Light morning — celebrate the week and set a clean reset heading into Monday.

Carry-Forward items to close out:
• Trailer field-readiness discussion — SCBA x4, muster stands x2, entrance signage, fire extinguisher: confirm corrective action closed
• Gipple 124H / 154H elevated H2S monitoring — confirm status or next crew plan
• Bizzell Irvin butterfly valve — confirm Orlando resolution plan complete
• SM Coonskin #152 4,000 ppm pull — confirm escalated or resolved
• Texas Ten #3610H (Caleb Smith) — confirm forklift and permit secured
exxonMoments:
Positive Observation — Well Conversion Executed Clean, Atmosphere Verified First (Stefanie Kirschke · June 17)
Air test completed and bleeders verified before work began. Crew followed procedures, confirmed zero energy, and maintained strong communication throughout — no rework required. Location left clean and equipment properly secured.
Lesson: Stage all equipment and materials before beginning work — verify in field, not on paper.

Positive Observation — Reconnect Completed Safely with Full Isolation Verification (Stefanie Kirschke · Texas Ten O'Neal 29q #218H · June 22)
Zero energy verified, atmosphere tested, all valves and bleeders confirmed functioning before reconnecting the well. Good communication between crews, isolation points verified, reconnect completed without incident. Location cleaned and organized on the way out.
Lesson: Walk the reconnect procedure and critical steps with all personnel before starting — it improves efficiency and catches gaps.

Positive Observation — Clean Job Execution Through Communication (Larry Jaramillo · Jordan N 23-24 4314H · June 17)
Job scope completed safely and as planned. Life saving rules discussed with all personnel. Strong communication between technician, PIC, and crew made job steps run smoothly. Crew also improved the location — removed rocks and evened out dirt around the wellhead.
Lesson: Inspect tools for wear and excessive dirt in hook and heel before each job.

fieldNews.headline: Louie Zarate — Client Validation at Emma Jane
fieldNews.detail: SM maintenance foreman Shane and HSE Colton visited Emma Jane and were 'really happy' with vessel washouts and location cleanliness after a month on site. Colton specifically called out Louie's JSA as 'very detailed' and told him to keep it up. That is external client validation — exactly what this company is built on.

fieldNews.headline: Brianda Sandoval — Multi-Location VRU Day
fieldNews.detail: McDaniel B Chriesman: 2 VRU units complete. Moved directly to Brynn 1-10H Battery for 1 VRU unit with electrical inspection. Clean execution across multiple sites in one shift.

fieldNews.headline: Jimmy White — Showed Up in Person
fieldNews.detail: Mike showed up personally at Williams Nail 5H #8H with Gage for AGT. The crew noticed. Leadership on the ground means something.

fieldNews.headline: Caleb Smith — Adapted Without Missing a Beat
fieldNews.detail: Shull Hanks 15C #203H rescheduled — workover rig in the work area. Caleb rerouted immediately to Bizzell Irvin, completed the job, then picked up Texas Ten #3610H reconnect to close out the day. No downtime, no drama.

todaysChallenge: Name one person this week who made your job easier.
todaysChallengeSubtext: Tell them. It takes 30 seconds and builds the kind of team people want to work on.

compliance.footerNote: Update numbers in daily.json each Monday morning.

fieldAlert.title: Trailer Went to the Field — Not Field Ready
fieldAlert.body: A trailer was dispatched to a job site this week that was not in field-ready condition. When a crew arrives on location without the required emergency equipment, we have removed their options if something goes wrong. This is a discussion item for this morning's meeting. What broke down in the check process, and what does the close-out look like going forward?
